purpose: F1 / OQ-12 MISSING-BLOCK acceptance oracle: qpAdm over a REAL-AADR f2 set extracted with maxmiss>0, so the f2 block array contains a PARTIALLY-covered block (a population pair with Vpair==0). AT2 read_f2(remove_na=TRUE) DROPS such a block ENTIRELY before the jackknife (keep=apply(f2,3,sum(!is.finite)==0)); steppe must reproduce the drop (NOT impute-0, which biases f4 toward 0 + inflates variance). The fit must match these to bit/tolerance parity per architecture.md S12 PARITY LAW + S13. UNLIKE every maxmiss=0 golden (no missing blocks), this exercises the est_to_loo_nafix path.

canonical_invocation: extract_f2(pref, outdir, pops=allpops, blgsize=0.05, maxmiss=0.99); f2b <- read_f2(outdir, pops=allpops); qpadm(f2b, left=left, right=right, target=target, boot=FALSE, getcov=TRUE, return_f4=TRUE, fudge=1e-4, cpp=TRUE)
note: CANONICAL = the f2-BLOCKS-OBJECT path qpadm(read_f2(dir), ...) — steppe's device-resident fit consumes a precomputed f2 tensor, so it is the analogue of qpadm(f2_object,...), NOT qpadm(dir,...) (the directory path applies a different internal f2-read arg set; on a sparse model the two diverge a lot — the documented golden_fit0 caveat). The committed fixture fixtures/f2_fitNA.bin is the RAW (pre-drop) f2 block tensor with the NA pair stored as IEEE-754 NaN; steppe derives Vpair (0 iff NaN) and reproduces AT2's keep-mask drop. The dropped block (0-based id 534) is the ONLY block with a Vpair==0 pair: Afghanistan_DarraiKurCave_MBA (pop idx 9) x Mbuti (pop idx 3). The 2-source model is a poor fit for these outgroups (the weights are large) — that is AT2's exact answer and steppe reproduces it bit-for-bit; the gate is steppe==AT2 WITH the drop, NOT a pretty fit. DISCRIMINATOR: the buggy impute-0 path gives weights [22.21207714, -21.21207714] (reldiff 1.45% from the drop) — above the tight tier, so the test fails impute-0.

description: steppe's internal f4 X (leftref=TARGET=England_BellBeaker, rows=sources, rightref=Mbuti) over the SURVIVING 709 blocks — what assemble_f4 computes (k = j + nr*i, i over rows, j over the 6 rights). This is steppe's parameterization (NOT AT2's source-ref f4); the GLS weights it yields equal AT2's qpadm(f2_object) weights above. The drop discriminator: the last column (Afghan_MBA) is the one a missing block most perturbs.
